C[genei.09: defending territory
beastin: Surround a group on all sides (so that there are no open spaces horizontally or vertically adjacent) and you take it.
beastin: The most fundamental thing about defending territory is about establishing
beastin: ""eyes""
genei.09: I've heard that term...doesn't seem to be working for me tho
beastin: Basically, you need a pair of open spots embedded among your pieces such that the opponent can't play in either spot (because he'd just die, being surrounded).
beastin: If you have that the group is safe.

beastin: A single open spot surrounded by your pieces isn't enough since if the opponent has surrounded all of the other pieces in a group then playing in the open spot kills your group not his piece.

C[beastin: ...what matters is chains.  (I just looked up the proper term.)  A chain is a set of stones that are connected rectilinearly, i.e. no diagonal connnections, like a boardwalk.
beastin: You have eight chains right now.
beastin: So you can have a group of chains that shares two eyes, but if one of the chains is only adjacent to one eye then I can surround that chain and then throw my piece in the eye to capture it.
